metadata.title: States PCS: Uttar Pradesh GK Questions for UPPSC Examinations

questions.question: The Sulh-i-Kul festival, celebrated in Agra, symbolized which of the following?

options.label: C
options.text: Commemoration of Urdu poet Mirza Ghalib

questions.explanation: Sulh-i-Kul, meaning “Peace with All,” was an important policy established by Mughal Emperor Akbar aimed at creating religious harmony and tolerance among different communities in his empire. The festival celebrated in Agra represents Akbar’s vision of bringing together people of diverse faiths, especially Hindus and Muslims, under a policy of mutual respect and peaceful coexistence. It became a symbol of unity, secularism, and inclusive governance during the Mughal era.

questions.question: What percent of Uttar Pradesh’s area is classified as forest area?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh has 6.88% of its total geographical area as recorded forest, mainly in Terai and Bhabar regions; this is below the national average forest cover.

questions.question: Which Bhadohi institute in Uttar Pradesh specializes in carpet technology?
questions.explanation: Indian Institute of Carpet Technology, Bhadohi, Uttar Pradesh, established in 2001, is Asia’s only institute dedicated exclusively to carpet technology and related research.

questions.question: What happened to Khan Bahadur Khan Rohilla after the 1857 revolt?

options.label: A
options.text: He was sentenced to life imprisonment.

options.label: B
options.text: He was captured by the British in Delhi.

options.label: C
options.text: He established his government in Lucknow.

options.label: D
options.text: He was handed over to the British by the Nepal army.

answer.label: D
answer.text: He was handed over to the British by the Nepal army.

questions.explanation: Khan Bahadur Khan fled to Nepal post-1857 revolt, but Nepalese forces captured and handed him to the British, leading to his execution.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the rulers of the Magadha Empire is incorrect?

options.label: A
options.text: Bimbisara conquered Anga to expand Magadha.

options.label: B
options.text: Ajatshatru was the son of Bimbisara.

options.label: C
options.text: Magadha’s rulers expanded their territory through wars with neighboring kingdoms.

options.label: D
options.text: Bimbisara conquered Kaushal and Avanti, which are located west and north-west of Bihar.

answer.label: D
answer.text: Bimbisara conquered Kaushal and Avanti, which are located west and north-west of Bihar.

questions.explanation: Bimbisara did not conquer Kaushal and Avanti; Ajatshatru ruled parts of Uttar Pradesh and both Kaushal and Avanti are west and north-west of Bihar.

questions.question: Who became the first Nawab of Awadh in 1722?
questions.explanation: Saadat Khan Nishapuri was the first Nawab of Awadh appointed in 1722. He established the Awadh dynasty, reformed administration, and strengthened the province’s military and finances, marking the beginning of semi-autonomous rule under Mughal suzerainty.

questions.question: Which city is the capital of Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Lucknow has served as the capital of Uttar Pradesh since 1921. It is the largest city in the state, renowned for its Nawabi heritage, and serves as the political, administrative, and cultural hub of Uttar Pradesh. Major institutions of governance are located here.

questions.question: What is the primary vegetable used in the traditional Uttar Pradesh dish Baigan Bharta?
questions.explanation: Baigan Bharta is made from mashed grilled eggplant mixed with spices and herbs, a staple in UP cuisine.

questions.question: Where was Rani Lakshmi Bai, the Queen of Jhansi, born?
questions.explanation: Born as Manikarnika in Varanasi in 1828, later became Rani Lakshmi Bai of Jhansi.

questions.question: Which unique architectural feature is most characteristic of Sharqi-style monuments found in Jaunpur, Uttar Pradesh?

options.label: D
options.text: Tall arched gateways with thick sloping walls

answer.label: D
answer.text: Tall arched gateways with thick sloping walls

questions.explanation: Sharqi-style monuments in Jaunpur are marked by tall arched gateways with thick sloping walls, projecting massive, fortress-like forms, as seen in Atala Masjid and Jama Masjid of the Sharqi dynasty.
